concerns.text: FR-012 states mediation analysis will be interpreted as 'associational' and acknowledges causality assumptions. However, the description of the mediation analysis (Baron & Kenny) doesn't explicitly mention the limitations of this approach in observational studies, particularly the potential for spurious associations due to unobserved confounders. While acknowledged generally, this needs more explicit discussion in the plan, as mediation analysis can be easily misinterpreted as implying causal pathways.

The plan relies on VIF to detect collinearity (FR-007). While VIF is a useful metric, it doesn't capture all forms of multicollinearity (e.g., non-linear relationships). A more comprehensive assessment of collinearity, including correlation matrices and potentially principal component analysis, might be warranted, especially given the potential for correlation between farm size and credit access as noted in the spec.
